title: SMB Share Enumeration
description: Enumerate Windows file shares on Metasploitable.
problemStatement:
# Objective
Use enumeration tools to discover SMB shares and access them anonymously.

## Steps
1.  Confirm SMB ports (139/445) are open using Nmap.
2.  Use `enum4linux` to perform a full enumeration of the SMB service. Save the output to a file.
3.  Use `smbclient` to list all available shares without providing a password.
4.  Connect to the `tmp` share (or any accessible share) and list its contents.
5.  (Bonus) Check if you can download any files.

## Deliverable
A list of the share names discovered and one file name found within an accessible share.
expectedOutput: Shares: `tmp`, `opt`, `www`. Inside `tmp` share: file `X` or empty.
solutionCode:
# Confirm SMB ports
nmap -p 139,445 192.168.56.101

# Full enum4linux
enum4linux -a 192.168.56.101 > enum4linux_output.txt

# List shares
smbclient -L //192.168.56.101 -N

# Connect to tmp share
smbclient //192.168.56.101/tmp -N
# Inside smbclient: ls, cd, get filename
